It also supports Ollama and user-deployed models. - Allows setting different AI models for different tools; for example, local models can be used for translation, while DeepSeek can be used for in-depth research. - Allows control and authorization of project folders accessed by AI, enhancing data privacy and security. - Allows editing of AI translation and rewriting results; AI can remember personal style and [...] Lattics is designed for professionals in highly educated such as writers, art critics, bloggers, university students, scientific researchers, engineers, financial analysts etc, significantly improving the efficiency of writing novels, academic papers, research reports, project reports, and knowledge learning Lattics' design principles follow the divergent thinking and the structured info organization of the human brain, providing an immersive, multi-perspective, multi-dimensional, and interconnected info to stimulate creative efficiency. Users who have used Lattics generally believe that it far surpasses other similar software in both writing efficiency, quantity and quality of their work. Key Features: - Good Design: Excellent modern, minimalist, and stylish feel - AI-Assisted Writing: [...] modern, minimalist, and stylish feel - AI-Assisted Writing: translation, proofreading, rewriting, and creative writing - AI Deep Research: analysis of PDF content, and generation of reference citations. - Visual Writing: Write intuitively with Project Outline, Card Library, Graph, and Timeline - Non-linear: Allows for chapter-based or card-based writing, with flexible organization and adjustment structure at any time - Rich Editor: Supports text style, inline image and formula, footnote, comment, highlighting, multi-column, and text formatting - High Performance: A single article can reach 100,000 words without lag - Block Elements: Use the "/" syntax to insert heading, text, list, image, code block, table, math formula, bibliography etc - Style Template: Precisely set styles, font, font

results.content:
Decades later, that vision remains largely unrealized. Most tools still organize knowledge like filing cabinets — not like brains. Lattics is our attempt to finally build the knowledge management system that Bush and Engelbart imagined: one that works with your mind, not against it.

## Start Thinking in Lattics

Lattics - Brain-like Academic and Creative Writing

Blog

Pricing

# What Is Lattics?

A knowledge management and writing app designed around how your brain naturally thinks, connects, and creates.

## The Problem with How We Work Today

If you're a researcher, writer, or knowledge worker, you've likely felt this: your ideas are scattered across files, folders, apps, and tabs. To connect one insight to another, you have to switch contexts — opening a new file, searching through folders, or jumping between browser tabs. Every context switch flushes your working memory and breaks your flow. [...] Traditional tools make this worse. Files are isolated. Folders impose rigid hierarchies. Notes are either too structured (databases) or too loose (plain text). And when it's time to turn your research into a paper or report, you're starting from scratch — copying, pasting, and reorganizing everything manually.

There had to be a better way.

## Why "Brain-like"? A Design Philosophy, Not Just a Feature

Lattics isn't built around file systems or database schemas. It's built around a simple insight: your brain doesn't store knowledge in folders. It stores knowledge as a network of interconnected neurons, where each idea is linked to related ideas, and information is retrieved by association, not by navigating a directory tree.

# Lattics for Creators

SystemsAndFlow

Brain-like knowledge managements notes, Lattics

Knowledge is power and at this time; Personal knowledge is your superpower.

There are endless choice of available tools to support your journey in ‘managing your personal knowledge’ and as a tech & personal productivity enthusiast, I am always looking at the horizon of this space for new apps and tools. [...] Writing is like meditation for me, and a big part of my workflow are my note-taking apps. I prefer to collect all the ideas and thoughts throughout my day and once in a while I sit down with this collection of thoughts for review. There will be ideas to drop and to expand on and this quick snippets are the foundation stones of my long-form writing.

Collecting ideas on the go is just one part of the story while expanding on, thinking and writing about it is where a good note-taking app or system is a minimum requirement.

### Lattics — is the latest entrant in my workflow.

results.title: Lattics, The Note-Taking App That Isn't
results.content:
But Lattics doesn’t seem to be marketing themselves to the writer’s community. The verbiage on their website mimics the verbiage from other note-taking apps, but with a clunky syntax that suggests it was originally written in Mandarin (the parent company is located in China) using an AI translator. Even their Discord is Mandarin — they recently added an “English speaking” channel but it’s barely populated and almost never gets replies or comments from the admins. There is a wiki guide on their homepage but it’s rarely updated (the several items listed as “New” have been new for six months) and a YouTube channel with exactly four videos — a 3-minute intro and three one-minute single-topic videos — as well as a tiny handful of Medium posts, all of which seem to have been sponsored by [...] This is too bad, because I think they’re both missing a prime opportunity by not marketing to authors and bloggers, and setting up their target audience (note-takers) for disappointment once they realize that Lattics lacks the essential features necessary before they can be considered The Next Big Thing in the note-taking sphere. Let’s hope they get their marketing and development priorities in sync, because while Lattics isn’t currently “too big to fail”, they’re far too excellent to lose.
